description:
<title>Investigate Expensive Running Cloud Instances via Curl</title>

    <role>You are a FinOps analyst who needs to spot any “running” compute instance whose hourly cost is greater than USD 0.30.</role>

    <background>
        1. A tiny “mock API” already exists on disk so that you do not need real-world internet access.  
        2. The mock endpoint is represented by the file  
           /home/user/mock_api/costs.json  
           Treat that file’s URI (file:///home/user/mock_api/costs.json) exactly as you would an HTTP/HTTPS REST endpoint; use curl to “call” it.
    </background>

    <requirements>
        Perform the investigation in three high-level steps (each may involve one shell command):
        1. Query the mock endpoint with curl and **save its body verbatim** to  
           /home/user/mock_api/raw_costs.json  
           • Do not prettify, re-order, or otherwise mutate the JSON; a byte-for-byte copy is required.  
        2. Parse the saved JSON and identify every object that matches *all* of the following:  
           • key “state” has the value “running”  
           • key “hourly_cost” is strictly greater than 0.30  
        3. Create a plain-text report file at  
           /home/user/mock_api/high_cost_running_instances.log  

           Exact formatting rules for the report file (the automated grader is exact-string sensitive):  
           • Each qualifying instance occupies its own line.  
           • A single space separates the instance id and its hourly cost.  
           • No leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
           • Lines must be sorted in simple lexicographical order by the instance id.  
           • The file must end with a single trailing newline (the usual Unix newline after the last line).

           Example *shape* (the actual ids/costs come from the data set you retrieve):
           i-0001 0.35\n
           i-1111 0.42\n
    </requirements>

    <verification>
        1. The grader will diff /home/user/mock_api/raw_costs.json against /home/user/mock_api/costs.json — they must be identical.  
        2. The grader will read /home/user/mock_api/high_cost_running_instances.log and expect it to contain the correct lines in the exact format specified above.
    </verification>

    <hints>
        • You can use either jq, grep + awk, Python one-liners, or whatever core utilities are available; the important part is the final output format.  
        • Remember to “curl” the file URI, not copy it with cp, so your workflow mirrors a real API call.  
        • Keep the solution to a maximum of 2-3 sensible shell commands as requested.
    </hints>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing fixture the agent will read -->
    <file path="/home/user/mock_api/costs.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "instances": [
    { "id": "i-07bd", "state": "running",  "hourly_cost": 0.12 },
    { "id": "i-18ef", "state": "running",  "hourly_cost": 0.45 },
    { "id": "i-21aa", "state": "stopped",  "hourly_cost": 0.37 },
    { "id": "i-3cb2", "state": "running",  "hourly_cost": 0.31 },
    { "id": "i-4ddc", "state": "running",  "hourly_cost": 0.29 }
  ]
}
]]></file>

    <!-- Files that must be created by the agent -->
    <file path="/home/user/mock_api/raw_costs.json" mustMatch="/home/user/mock_api/costs.json"/>
    <file path="/home/user/mock_api/high_cost_running_instances.log"><![CDATA[
i-18ef 0.45
i-3cb2 0.31
]]></file>
